explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey, um, I was reading about free will the other day. You know, like, do we really have it or is everything determined? What do you think? Oh man, free will? Yeah, that's a big one. I lean towards it being an illusion, like, compatibilism or whatever. Nah, actually hard determinism. But hit me with your take. Well, I mean, my philosophy prof, Dr. Ramirez, he's super into libertarian free will. He says we have genuine choices that aren't determined by prior causes. Okay, but why? Like, what's his reasoning? Physics says everything's cause and effect, right? Quantum stuff maybe, but even that's probabilistic, not free. Uh, yeah, he talks about that. But he says the tradition from, like, Descartes and Kant holds that moral responsibility requires real free will. Without it, blame and praise don't make sense.
